stories.title: Kostyuk def. Svitolina 6-3, 2-6, 6-2 — First Ukrainian Woman in Roland Garros SF

x_posts.label: Post 1-1
x_posts.text: Kostyuk def. Svitolina 6-3, 2-6, 6-2. First Ukrainian woman in a Roland Garros semifinal in the Open Era. She beat Swiatek in the R16. Now Svitolina. History at every stage of this run. #RolandGarros #TennisFanrecap #FrenchOpen #Kostyuk

x_posts.label: Post 1-2
x_posts.text: Kostyuk arrived at Roland Garros on a 16-match clay winning streak. She's now beaten Swiatek AND Svitolina — the No. 3 seed and Rome champion — in back-to-back rounds. Into the last four. #RolandGarros #TennisFanrecap #FrenchOpen #WTA

facebook.long_form:
**Window:** 8:00 AM ET

Marta Kostyuk has just made history at Roland Garros.

The 23-year-old Ukrainian defeated compatriot Elina Svitolina 6-3, 2-6, 6-2 in the first all-Ukrainian Grand Slam quarterfinal in the Open Era, becoming the first Ukrainian woman ever to reach a Roland Garros semifinal.

It wasn't straightforward. Svitolina — the Rome champion who arrived in Paris on a 10-match clay winning streak — battled back to level the match at one set all. The deciding set was extraordinary: five successive breaks of serve before Kostyuk seized control at 3-2 and closed it out.

What makes this run so remarkable is who Kostyuk had to beat to get here. In the fourth round she defeated four-time Roland Garros champion Iga Swiatek, 7-5, 6-1. Then she knocked out the No. 7 seed and Rome title-holder. She arrived in Paris on a 16-match clay winning streak, and she has not stopped yet.

On Thursday, Kostyuk faces No. 8 seed Mirra Andreeva in the women's semifinal.

Are you watching this Kostyuk run?

---
facebook.image_caption:
**Window:** 8:30 AM ET

HISTORY MADE.

Marta Kostyuk is the first Ukrainian woman in a Roland Garros semifinal in the Open Era. She beat Swiatek in the fourth round. Svitolina in the quarterfinal. Next up: Andreeva on Thursday.

article.headline: Kostyuk Makes History, Beats Svitolina to Become First Ukrainian Woman in Roland Garros Semifinal

stories.title: Mensik def. Fonseca 6-4, 6-3, 7-6(3) — Into First Major Semifinal

x_posts.label: Post 2-1
x_posts.text: Mensik def. Fonseca 6-4, 6-3, 7-6(3). He converted the SEVENTH match point after losing six in a row. Under a closed roof on Chatrier. Ice cold. First man born in 2004 or later to reach a major SF. #RolandGarros #TennisFanrecap #FrenchOpen #Mensik

x_posts.label: Post 2-2
x_posts.text: Mensik (20) beat the teenager who beat Djokovic and Ruud to face Zverev in the Roland Garros SF. The youngest Czech in a GS QF since Ivan Lendl. Generation 2004 has arrived on the biggest stage. #RolandGarros #TennisFanrecap #FrenchOpen #NextGen

facebook.long_form:
**Window:** 12:30 PM ET

The score was 6-5 in the third set. Jakub Mensik had his chance. Then he had another. Then another. Six match points in one agonizing return game — all lost.

Then he went into the tiebreak and won it 7-3 without a trace of nerves.

Mensik defeated Joao Fonseca 6-4, 6-3, 7-6(3) in their all-teen Roland Garros quarterfinal under the closed roof of Court Philippe-Chatrier, converting his seventh match point to become the first man born in 2004 or later to reach a Grand Slam semifinal.

He is 20 years old. He is the youngest Czech in a Grand Slam quarterfinal since Ivan Lendl in 1980. And he just beat a player who had already knocked out Novak Djokovic and Casper Ruud at this very tournament.

Roland Garros called him the "ice-man." After what we saw in that tiebreak, it's hard to argue. On Thursday, Mensik faces No. 2 seed Alexander Zverev for a place in the final.

What was your reaction watching those match points go by?

---
facebook.image_caption:
**Window:** 1:30 PM ET

Mensik. 20 years old. 7th match point. Done.

First man born in 2004 or later to reach a Grand Slam semifinal. Roland Garros 2026 is producing some all-time moments.

article.headline: Mensik Converts Seventh Match Point to Reach First Grand Slam Semifinal at Roland Garros

stories.title: Chwalinska def. Kalinskaya 7-6(3), 6-3 — Qualifier Into Maiden Grand Slam SF

x_posts.label: Post 3-1
x_posts.text: Qualifier Chwalinska def. No. 22 seed Kalinskaya 7-6(3), 6-3. Into the Roland Garros semifinal. Only the 2nd women's qualifier to reach a Grand Slam SF in the Open Era, after Podoroska here in 2020. #RolandGarros #TennisFanrecap #FrenchOpen #Chwalinska

x_posts.label: Post 3-2
x_posts.text: Chwalinska has won 8 matches in a row — 3 in qualifying, 5 in the main draw. She's dropped one set all tournament. Now she's in a Roland Garros semifinal. The best kind of tennis story. #RolandGarros #TennisFanrecap #FrenchOpen #WTA

facebook.long_form:
**Window:** 10:00 AM ET

Let's talk about Maja Chwalinska for a moment.

She came to Roland Garros as a qualifier — which means she had to win three matches just to get into the main draw. Then she had to win five more to reach the quarterfinals. And this morning, on Court Philippe-Chatrier, she defeated No. 22 seed Anna Kalinskaya 7-6(3), 6-3 to reach the Roland Garros semifinal.

Eight wins in a row. One set dropped in the entire tournament. At 24, in her maiden Grand Slam semifinal appearance.

Chwalinska is just the second women's qualifier in the Open Era to reach a Grand Slam semifinal — the other was Nadia Podoroska, who did it at this very tournament in 2020. There is something about Roland Garros that unlocks extraordinary runs.

On Thursday, she will face the winner of today's quarterfinal between world No. 1 Aryna Sabalenka and Diana Shnaider. Sabalenka will be the heavy favourite, but Maja Chwalinska has spent this entire tournament proving she belongs.

Who's watching her story unfold?

---
facebook.image_caption:
**Window:** 10:30 AM ET

Qualifier. 8 wins in a row. Roland Garros semifinal.

Maja Chwalinska is only the 2nd women's qualifier to reach a Grand Slam SF in the Open Era. The dream run continues on Thursday.

article.headline: Chwalinska Becomes Second Women's Qualifier in Open Era to Reach Grand Slam Semifinal

stories.title: Day 11 QF Watch — Sabalenka, Auger-Aliassime, All-Italian Night Session

x_posts.label: Post 4
x_posts.text: Day 11 watch list: Sabalenka vs Shnaider is underway. FAA vs Cobolli to follow. Tonight: Berrettini vs Arnaldi — all-Italian Chatrier night session (not before 8:15 PM Paris). Three Italians in one GS QF draw. #RolandGarros #TennisFanrecap #FrenchOpen

facebook.long_form:
**Window:** 1:00 PM ET

Roland Garros Day 11 is serving up history from the first match to the last.

We started this morning with Chwalinska's quarterfinal win over Kalinskaya. Then Aryna Sabalenka — No. 1 seed, no sets dropped all tournament — stepped onto Chatrier against left-handed Diana Shnaider, the No. 25 seed who defeated Madison Keys to reach her maiden Grand Slam quarterfinal.

This afternoon, No. 4 Felix Auger-Aliassime faces No. 10 Flavio Cobolli for a place in the men's semifinal.

And tonight? On Philippe-Chatrier, not before 8:15 PM Paris time, there is an all-Italian quarterfinal: Matteo Berrettini vs Matteo Arnaldi. Three Italian men reached the Roland Garros quarterfinals this year — Cobolli, Berrettini, and Arnaldi — the first time in Open Era history that three Italians have gone this deep in the same major.

It is a remarkable day at Roland Garros. Is everyone watching?

---
facebook.image_caption:
**Window:** 1:30 PM ET

Day 11 on Philippe-Chatrier: Sabalenka, Auger-Aliassime, and tonight — an all-Italian QF.

Three Italians in the same Grand Slam quarterfinals for the first time in the Open Era. Roland Garros 2026 keeps delivering.

article.headline: Sabalenka Faces Left-Handed Qualifier Shnaider in Day 11 Quarterfinal Amid Historic Italian Night Session

x_posts.label: Post 5
x_posts.text: Cobolli. Berrettini. Arnaldi. Three Italian men in the same Grand Slam QF — first time in the Open Era. Berrettini vs Arnaldi on Chatrier tonight. One of them reaches the Roland Garros semifinal. #RolandGarros #TennisFanrecap #FrenchOpen #ItalianTennis

facebook.long_form:
**Window:** 6:00 PM ET

Italian tennis has never had a day quite like this at Roland Garros.

Flavio Cobolli reached the quarterfinals as the No. 10 seed. Matteo Berrettini — a former Wimbledon finalist making his first Grand Slam quarterfinal appearance in five years — followed him. Then Matteo Arnaldi, who ended Frances Tiafoe's tournament in a five-hour-26-minute five-set epic that many called the match of the tournament.

Three Italian men. Same Grand Slam quarterfinal. First time in the Open Era.

Tonight, two of them meet on Court Philippe-Chatrier: Berrettini vs Arnaldi. One Italian man will be in the Roland Garros semifinal. The other goes home knowing he was part of something extraordinary.

As for the rest of the men's draw: Zverev meets Mensik in one semifinal — the No. 2 seed against the 20-year-old Czech who became the first man of his generation to reach a major semifinal. The other semi will feature the Italian survivor against the winner of Auger-Aliassime vs Cobolli.

Women's semifinals on Thursday: Kostyuk vs Andreeva, and Chwalinska vs today's Sabalenka/Shnaider winner.

Who do you have making the finals?

---
facebook.image_caption:
**Window:** 6:30 PM ET

Cobolli. Berrettini. Arnaldi.

Three Italians in one Grand Slam QF — the first time ever in the Open Era. Tonight's Berrettini vs Arnaldi is all-Italian on Philippe-Chatrier. This is Italian tennis's greatest day at Roland Garros.

article.headline: Three Italians in the Roland Garros Quarterfinals: An Open Era First for Italian Tennis
